title: Storage, Filesystems, Archives, and Backups · Operations Case: Mount Observation Safety
summary: Respond to low disk space and protect service data without confusing filesystems, mounts, archives, snapshots, and verified backups. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux mount observation safety increment in a storage map and restore-tested backup plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use lsblk, findmnt, mount, and filesystem identifiers to observe topology before any mount or format action.
Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Mount Observation Safety
steps.instruction:
theory case — Filesystem / is 92% full with 500 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Mount Observation Safety contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use lsblk, findmnt, mount, and filesystem identifiers to observe topology before any mount or format action.
